Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is to make the middle and bottom rows match each other (not the top row). Let me check if any shift of the bottom row matches the middle row.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if any shift of bottom row matches middle row
mid_blocks = ['pp', 'gg', 'ww', 'gg', 'gg', 'ww', 'bb', 'bb', 'gg', 'SS']
bot_blocks = ['SS', 'ww', 'ww', 'bb', 'SS', 'ww', 'bb', 'gg', 'SS', 'ww']

print("Checking if any shift of bottom matches middle:")
for shift in range(len(bot_blocks)):
    shifted = bot_blocks[shift:] + bot_blocks[:shift]
    if shifted == mid_blocks:
        print(f"  MATCH at shift {shift}!")
    else:
        # Count matching positions
        matches = sum(1 for a, b in zip(shifted, mid_blocks) if a == b)
        print(f"  Shift {shift}: {shifted} vs {mid_blocks} -> {matches}/10 match")

# Also check if any shift of middle matches bottom
print("\nChecking if any shift of middle matches bottom:")
for shift in range(len(mid_blocks)):
    shifted = mid_blocks[shift:] + mid_blocks[:shift]
    if shifted == bot_blocks:
        print(f"  MATCH at shift {shift}!")
    else:
        matches = sum(1 for a, b in zip(shifted, bot_blocks) if a == b)
        print(f"  Shift {shift}: {shifted} vs {bot_blocks} -> {matches}/10 match")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
